content.text: docker:mom-sandbox
/home/jpolo/Vault/Knowledge Base

I want to do a short research stay at an european university research group as part of an Erasmus+ project. I want to do this before starting my phd, like an apprenticeship so I can get knowledge from other research groups that are more familiar with the topic so I can go back and do that in my own research group in my university. I want to orient my phd in the broad topic of agentic and multi modal AI systems in the context of cyber ranges or ed tech. My research grouop is already an expert in cyber ranges and edTech, but we do not have expertise in Agentic and multimodal AI, especially agentic AI and llms. I want you to do a web search to investigate which research groups in different european universities could fit for my stay so I can learn from them. Look about what they publish, and write a page in a new directory called internship/ in the vault. In fact, create one page per proposed institution, with all the information, publications, etc. and a central dashboard

The user wants me to research European university research groups working on agentic AI and multimodal AI systems, particularly in contexts that could relate to cyber ranges or ed tech. They want to do an Erasmus+ research stay before their PhD. I need to:

1. Search for relevant European research groups
2. Find details about their publications, focus areas
3. Create an `internship/` directory in the vault with individual pages per institution and a central dashboard

Let me start by doing web searches for relevant research groups across multiple dimensions.

The user wants me to research European university research groups focused on agentic AI, multimodal AI, and LLMs - particularly those that could complement their existing expertise in cyber ranges and ed tech. I need to:

1. Do web searches to find relevant research groups
2. Investigate their publications and focus areas
3. Create an `internship/` directory in the Knowledge Base vault with individual pages per institution and a central dashboard

Title: Agentic Systems Lab - ETH Zürich

URL Source: https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/

Markdown Content:
# Agentic Systems Lab - ETH Zürich

[![Image 1: Agentic Systems Lab - ETH Zurich](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/logos/ETHxASL_Logo.svg?dpl=dpl_6TSRWp7MTZFiaudTieiJbtbSv9Ha)](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/)

[Lab Focus](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/#lab-focus)[Opportunities](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/#opportunities)[Partners](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/#partners)[Events](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/#events)[Team](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/#team)[Contact](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/#contact)

![Image 2: Agentic Systems Lab](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/asl-logo-white.svg?dpl=dpl_6TSRWp7MTZFiaudTieiJbtbSv9Ha)

# We enable the next generation of AI builders.

We design, develop, and evaluate AI systems that run the real world, and shape the researchers and founders who will define its future. Based at ETH Zurich.

[Explore Research](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/#lab-focus)[Join the Lab](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/#opportunities)

Lab Focus

## Designing, developing, and evaluating AI systems in real-world environments.

Our projects span industry verticals and fundamental research, always grounded in practical impact.

[01 ### Agentic AI We build and evaluate agentic AI applications across a variety of industries, from insurance and finance to education and scientific research. Learn More](https://agenticsystemslab.notion.site/Agentic-AI-Projects-320b0f8bfec080cca8cad20d25de26b6?source=copy_link)

[02 ### Multimodal AI We develop Time-Series Language Models (TSLMs) and Audio-Language Models (ALMs) to reason over and predict temporal data in healthcare, manufacturing, telecommunications, and energy. Learn More](https://agenticsystemslab.notion.site/Multimodal-AI-Projects-314b0f8bfec08057bed9d6d7cf4d42b4?source=copy_link)

[03 ### Next-Gen RAG Next-Gen RAG systems that autonomously plan and optimize retrieval. AutoRAG configures pipelines automatically; MetaRAG orchestrates multiple strategies for reliable, grounded outputs. Learn More](https://agenticsystemslab.notion.site/Next-Gen-RAG-Projects-320b0f8bfec080fc94d5d05378f1ba2b?source=copy_link)

[04 ### World Models We research world models that learn compressed representations of environments, enabling AI systems to simulate, plan, and reason about complex real-world dynamics. Learn More](https://agenticsystemslab.notion.site/World-Model-Projects-320b0f8bfec080e69005f8834c91c383?source=copy_link)

[05 ### AI Evaluation Frameworks Creating benchmarks and methodologies to measure performance, safety, and reliability of AI Agents and multimodal LLMs. Learn More](https://agenticsystemslab.notion.site/AI-Evaluation-Frameworks-320b0f8bfec08009b913ea0479068584?source=copy_link)

[06 ### AI Transformation We research how AI can transform existing enterprise operations. Our work spans user discovery and problem validation to ensure meaningful applications of AI, as well as the design of aligned and controllable AI systems that integrate reliably into real-world workflows. Learn More](https://agenticsystemslab.notion.site/AI-Transformation-320b0f8bfec0801f9f8cdb8f4be3e306?source=copy_link)

Student & Researcher Opportunities

## Join us for your thesis, semester, or research project.

Work on AI with real-world impact across four tracks.

[For students ### AI Research A predoctoral program designed to turn your thesis into a top-tier CS conference publication with you as first author. Learn More](https://agenticsystemslab.notion.site/AI-Research-Track-Student-Applications-248b0f8bfec080c4833bda116f043078?source=copy_link)

[For students ### AI Transformation Work alongside our startup and industry partners to bring AI applications into leading companies. Learn More](https://agenticsystemslab.notion.site/AI-Transformation-Track-Student-Applications-246b0f8bfec0805cad99d73be6f4d43a?source=copy_link)

[For students ### AI Founder Already working on your own AI idea? Turn your thesis or semester project into a launchpad for your venture. Learn More](https://agenticsystemslab.notion.site/AI-Founder-Track-Student-Applications-260b0f8bfec080798e06c9d336f7024a?source=copy_link)

[For researchers ### Affiliate Researcher For PhD, postdoc, and industry researchers: Pursue collaborative AI research projects with autonomy and mentorship. Learn More](https://agenticsystemslab.notion.site/Affiliate-Visiting-Researcher-Track-2c9b0f8bfec0807f9021caf74758cc5a?source=copy_link)

Startup & Industry Partnerships

## Collaborate with ETH Zurich to develop practical AI solutions.

Join our Startup and Industry partners who are pioneering applications of agentic AI and multimodal LLMs. Discover what works, when, and why with some of Europe's brightest and most ambitious minds.

[Start a Partnership Discussion](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/#contact)

Core Industry Partners

[![Image 3: Zurich Insurance](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/logos/Zurich_Insurance_Group_Logo_Horizontal.svg.png?dpl=dpl_6TSRWp7MTZFiaudTieiJbtbSv9Ha)Research Partner for Agentic AI in Insurance](https://www.zurich.com/media/news-releases/2025/2025-1029-01)

[![Image 21: Taskbase](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/logos/Taskbase.svg?dpl=dpl_6TSRWp7MTZFiaudTieiJbtbSv9Ha)Offers joint student projects](https://taskbase.com/)

[![Image 22: Studyflash](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/logos/Studyflash.svg?dpl=dpl_6TSRWp7MTZFiaudTieiJbtbSv9Ha)Offers joint student projects](https://www.studyflash.ai/)

[![Image 23: Forgis](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/logos/Forgis.png?dpl=dpl_6TSRWp7MTZFiaudTieiJbtbSv9Ha)Offers joint student projects](https://www.forgis.com/)

Events

## Bringing together builders and researchers.

We organize hackathons, workshops, and collaborative events that connect students, researchers, and industry to explore the frontiers of AI.

[February 28, 2026 ### Agentic Hack 2026 A one-day hackathon in Zurich focused on building agentic AI systems, co-hosted with Google DeepMind, Vercel, Founderful, and Mixedbread. Teams prototyped applications with optional access to robotic platforms from ZHAW CAI. With ![Image 24: Google DeepMind](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/logos/Google%20Deepmind.png?dpl=dpl_6TSRWp7MTZFiaudTieiJbtbSv9Ha) ![Image 25: Founderful](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/logos/founderful.svg?dpl=dpl_6TSRWp7MTZFiaudTieiJbtbSv9Ha) ![Image 26: Vercel](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/logos/Vercel.svg?dpl=dpl_6TSRWp7MTZFiaudTieiJbtbSv9Ha) ![Image 27: mixedbread](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/logos/mixedbread-transparent.png?dpl=dpl_6TSRWp7MTZFiaudTieiJbtbSv9Ha) ![Image 28: Studyflash](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/logos/Studyflash.svg?dpl=dpl_6TSRWp7MTZFiaudTieiJbtbSv9Ha) View Event](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/events/agentic-hack-2026)

[February 21, 2026 ### Supercell x AI Event A fireside chat at ETH Zurich with Supercell's AI Innovation Lab and Founderful on the future of AI in gaming, followed by live demos from builders across the ETH ecosystem. With ![Image 29: Supercell](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/logos/Supercell.png?dpl=dpl_6TSRWp7MTZFiaudTieiJbtbSv9Ha) ![Image 30: Founderful](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/logos/founderful.svg?dpl=dpl_6TSRWp7MTZFiaudTieiJbtbSv9Ha) ![Image 31: ETH Game Dev Club](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/logos/ethgdc.png?dpl=dpl_6TSRWp7MTZFiaudTieiJbtbSv9Ha) View Event](https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/events/supercell-ai-event)

Our Team

## Researchers, engineers, and builders.

We have launched companies, software, rockets, and conducted research at world-leading institutions. Agency, ownership, trust and collaboration is part of our DNA.

# Adaptive & Agentic AI (A3 Lab)

[](https://ece.au.dk/)

### A3 Lab (Adaptive & Agentic AI) develops trustworthy adaptive and agentic AI—especially test-time adaptation and multimodal foundation models—for safe deployment under domain shift in safety-critical real-world applications.

[](https://ece.au.dk/)

A3 Lab is a research group at Aarhus University advancing **adaptive and agentic AI** for reliable deployment under non-stationarity. We develop multimodal foundation models and test-time learning methods that can quantify uncertainty, and adapt safely on the fly—minimizing costly re-training while maintaining robustness, auditability, and governance in real-world, safety-critical settings.

[](https://ece.au.dk/)

![Image 2](https://ece.au.dk/fileadmin/ece/Research_pillars_of_A3_Lab.jpeg)

 Research pillars of A3 Lab: Vision. 

[](https://ece.au.dk/)
### Vision–Language Learning & Reasoning

We study **multimodal foundation models** (vision-language and beyond) for robust perception and decision making under distribution shift. Our work includes **prompting and instruction following**, **interactive and agentic systems**, and **explainability and faithful reasoning** so that outputs are not only accurate but also verifiable.

### Self-Supervised Representation Learning

We develop **self-supervised learning** methods to build stronger representations that transfer across tasks and domains. We also explore **synthetic data and generative modeling** to improve robustness, cover rare conditions, and reduce dependence on labeled data.

[](https://ece.au.dk/)
### Open-World, Continual & Test-Time Adaptation

We design lightweight methods that update models at inference time using unlabeled or weak supervision. Key topics include **domain generalization / test-time adaptation**, **parameter-efficient continual updates**, and **unknown-class / novelty recognition** to handle evolving and recurring domain shifts.

### Multi-Agent Orchestration & Efficient Vision

We investigate **multi-agent coordination and planning** for complex AI pipelines, including **human–AI teaming** and decision support. To enable practical deployment, we work on **efficient model design** (distillation, efficient transformers, TinyML-style constraints) and rigorous evaluation protocols for reliability under drift.

[](https://ece.au.dk/)

Title: Language and Multimodal AI Lab

URL Source: https://lama.doc.ic.ac.uk/

Markdown Content:
![Image 1](https://lama.doc.ic.ac.uk/imgs/main.jpg)

#### Language and Multimodal AI Lab (LAMA)

The **Language and Multimodal AI Lab (LAMA)** aims to develop machine learning algorithms for language understanding and generation following a holistic approach where language can be combined with other types of information. In addition to textual context of varying length, our models include knowledge in the form of images, videos, audio recordings, sensor data, metadata and knowledge bases. Multimodal machine learning is therefore at the core of our research. The main areas we work on include (multimodal) language generation, machine translation, text adaptation, image captioning, transfer learning for language, NLP for health and sustainable development, quality evaluation and estimation, and language learning applications.

* * *

#### Recent Publications

[* Logically Consistent Adversarial Attacks for Soft Theorem Provers](https://lama.doc.ic.ac.uk/)*   Alexander Gaskell, Yishu Miao, Francesca Toni, Lucia Specia
*   IJCAI-ECAI 2022
[* Guiding Visual Question Generation](https://lama.doc.ic.ac.uk/)*   Nihir Vedd, Zixu Wang, Marek Rei, Yishu Miao, Lucia Specia
*   NAACL 2022
[* SURF: Semantic-level Unsupervised Reward Function for Machine Translation](https://lama.doc.ic.ac.uk/)*   Atijit Anuchitanukul, Julia Ive
*   NAACL 2022
[* Leveraging Pre-trained Language Models for Gender Debiasing](https://lama.doc.ic.ac.uk/)*   Nishtha Jain, Maja Popovic, Declan Groves, Lucia Specia
*   LREC 2022
[* Multilingual and Multimodal Learning for Brazilian Portuguese](https://lama.doc.ic.ac.uk/)*   Júlia Sato, Helena Caseli, Lucia Specia
*   LREC 2022
[* MLQE-PE: A Multilingual Quality Estimation and Post-Editing Dataset](https://lama.doc.ic.ac.uk/)*   Marina Fomicheva, Shuo Sun, Erick Fonseca, Chrysoula Zerva, Frederic Blain, Vishrav Chaudhary, Francisco Guzman, Nina Lopatina, Andre F. T. Martins, Lucia Specia
*   LREC 2022
[* MultiSubs: A Large-scale Multimodal and Multilingual Dataset](https://lama.doc.ic.ac.uk/)*   Josiah Wang, Josiel Figueiredo, Lucia Specia
*   LREC 2022

#### News and Highlights

*   **22/04/2022:**1 paper accepted to IJCAI-ECAI 2022.
*   **08/04/2022:**2 papers accepted to NAACL 2022.
*   **06/04/2022:**4 papers accepted to LREC 2022.
*   **23/02/2022:**2 papers accepted to ACL 2022.
*   **30/11/2021:**1 paper accepted to AAAI 2022.
*   **15/10/2021:**1 paper accepted to BMVC 2021.
*   **25/08/2021:**3 papers accepted to EMNLP(Findings) and EMNLP (Demo) 2021.
*   **05/05/2021:**4 papers accepted to ACL and ACL(Findings) 2021.
*   **10/03/2021:**2 papers accepted to NAACL 2021.
*   **14/01/2021:**4 papers accepted to EACL 2021.
*   **08/12/2020:**[Curious Case of Language Generation Evaluation Metrics: A Cautionary Tale](https://www.aclweb.org/anthology/2020.coling-main.210/) to appear at COLING 2020.
*   **04/12/2020:**[An Exploratory Study on Multilingual Quality Estimation](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nDfKtnUtZtQ) to appear at AACL 2020.
*   **21/11/2020:**Our lab is online!

arguments.content:
# Erasmus+ Research Stay — Candidate Institutions

> **Goal:** Short research stay (pre-PhD apprenticeship) at a European university to acquire expertise in **agentic AI / multimodal AI / LLMs**, to bring back and apply in the context of **cyber ranges and ed tech**.

## Selection Criteria

| Criterion | Weight |
|---|---|
| Direct work on agentic AI or LLM-based agents | ★★★ |
| Multimodal AI (vision-language, audio-language, etc.) | ★★★ |
| Intersection with cybersecurity / cyber ranges / CTF | ★★☆ |
| Intersection with education technology / intelligent tutoring | ★★☆ |
| Erasmus+ agreement feasibility (EU/EEA country) | ★★☆ |
| Group size and mentorship capacity | ★☆☆ |
| Publication quality and recentness | ★★☆ |

## Summary Table

| # | Institution | Group | Country | Primary Fit | Erasmus+ |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **ETH Zürich** | Agentic Systems Lab | Switzerland | Agentic AI, Multimodal, Education | Non-EU but participates |
| 2 | **Aarhus University** | A3 Lab — Adaptive & Agentic AI | Denmark | Agentic AI, Multimodal, Test-time Adaptation | EU ✓ |
| 3 | **SDU Odense** | Agentic AI & LLM Evaluation Lab | Denmark | Agentic AI evaluation, Digital Twins | EU ✓ |
| 4 | **TU Darmstadt** | Multimodal AI Lab (MAI) | Germany | Multimodal Grounded & Reliable AI | EU ✓ |
| 5 | **Imperial College London** | LAMA Lab | UK | Multimodal NLP, Language+Vision | Post-Brexit, check eligibility |
| 6 | **University of Twente** | Pervasive Systems + IST | Netherlands | CTF multi-agent, AI in Education | EU ✓ |
| 7 | **CISPA Helmholtz (Saarland)** | Various groups | Germany | AI + Cybersecurity, LLM agents for security | EU ✓ |
| 8 | **University of Würzburg** | Data Science Chair | Germany | BRACE-LLM (blue/red agents for cybersecurity) | EU ✓ |
| 9 | **Utrecht University** | AI in Education Lab | Netherlands | LLMs for computing education | EU ✓ |
| 10 | **University of Tübingen / IWM** | AI2Teach + Language & AI in Education | Germany | AI for education, language tutoring | EU ✓ |
| 11 | **University of Bern** | Software Engineering Group | Switzerland | Agentic LLMs for SE | Non-EU but participates |
| 12 | **Know-Center / TU Graz** | IACAI — Agent-Centric AI | Austria | Agent-centric AI, interfaces | EU ✓ |
| 13 | **University of Edinburgh** | Autonomous Agents + ELIAI | UK | Agentic AI, LLM planning, uncertainty | Post-Brexit, check eligibility |

## Detailed Pages

Each institution has a dedicated page with full details:

- [[01 - ETH Zürich — Agentic Systems Lab]]
- [[02 - Aarhus University — A3 Lab]]
- [[03 - SDU Odense — Agentic AI & LLM Evaluation Lab]]
- [[04 - TU Darmstadt — Multimodal AI Lab]]
- [[05 - Imperial College London — LAMA Lab]]
- [[06 - University of Twente — Pervasive Systems & IST]]
- [[07 - CISPA Helmholtz — AI & Cybersecurity]]
- [[08 - University of Würzburg — BRACE-LLM]]
- [[09 - Utrecht University — AI in Education Lab]]
- [[10 - University of Tübingen / IWM — AI2Teach]]
- [[11 - University of Bern — Software Engineering Group]]
- [[12 - Know-Center Graz — IACAI]]
- [[13 - University of Edinburgh — Autonomous Agents & ELIAI]]

## Top Picks (Recommended)

Based on fit with your profile (cyber ranges + ed tech background, need agentic/multimodal AI expertise):

1. **ETH Zürich — Agentic Systems Lab** — Closest match. Explicit "Agentic AI in Education" track, strong on multimodal, has an Affiliate Researcher track for visiting researchers. Switzerland has bilateral agreements with Erasmus+ programme countries.
2. **University of Twente — Pervasive Systems + IST** — Direct intersection: they work on CTF-solving with multi-agent systems AND AI in education (CLAIR project). Excellent complementarity with your cyber range expertise.
3. **Aarhus University — A3 Lab** — Strong agentic AI focus, multimodal foundation models, test-time adaptation. Clean Erasmus+ eligibility. Good size for mentorship.
4. **CISPA Helmholtz** — If cybersecurity is the dominant angle, CISPA's work on LLM agents in security contexts (breaking autonomous agents, CTI) directly bridges your domain.
5. **SDU Odense** — Dual lab structure (Agentic AI Evaluation + Digital Twin/Agentic AI Infrastructure) gives both theoretical and infrastructure expertise. Denmark is very Erasmus+-friendly.

## Next Steps

- [ ] Verify Erasmus+ inter-institutional agreements with your home university for each candidate
- [ ] Contact potential supervisors (see individual pages for leads)
- [ ] Prepare a 1-page research proposal bridging your cyber range expertise with the host's agentic AI work
- [ ] Check Erasmus+ application deadlines (typically 6-12 months before the stay)

arguments.content:
# ETH Zürich — Agentic Systems Lab

## Overview

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Institution** | ETH Zürich (Eidgenössische Technische Hochschule) |
| **Lab** | Agentic Systems Lab |
| **Website** | https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/ |
| **Country** | Switzerland |
| **Erasmus+** | Non-EU, but Switzerland participates via bilateral agreements with Erasmus+ programme countries |
| **Department** | Management, Technology, and Economics (MTEC) |

## Lab Directors

- **Dr. Robert Jakob** — Founder & Co-Director, Postdoc ETH
- **Dr. Kevin O'Sullivan** — Founder & Co-Director, Postdoc ETH
- **Prof. Dr. Elgar Fleisch** — Professor, ETH Zurich & University of St. Gallen

## Research Focus Areas

The lab organizes its work into six pillars:

### 1. Agentic AI
Build and evaluate agentic AI applications across industries including **insurance, finance, education, and scientific research**. This is the core pillar most relevant to your PhD direction.

### 2. Multimodal AI
Develop **Time-Series Language Models (TSLMs)** and **Audio-Language Models (ALMs)** to reason over and predict temporal data in healthcare, manufacturing, telecommunications, and energy.

### 3. Next-Gen RAG
Autonomous retrieval-augmented generation: AutoRAG (automatic pipeline configuration), MetaRAG (multi-strategy orchestration for grounded outputs).

### 4. World Models
Compressed representations of environments enabling AI systems to simulate, plan, and reason about complex real-world dynamics.

### 5. AI Evaluation Frameworks
Benchmarks and methodologies to measure performance, safety, and reliability of AI agents and multimodal LLMs.

### 6. AI Transformation
How AI transforms enterprise operations — user discovery, problem validation, aligned and controllable AI systems integrated into real-world workflows.

## Key People for Your Interest

- **Maximilian May** — PhD Researcher, "Agentic AI in Education" (HSG) — direct overlap with your ed tech interest
- **Dr. Fan Wu** — Postdoc, Multimodal AI in Healthcare
- **Dr. Patrick Langer** — AI Research Lead, AI Research Scientist
- **Dr. Kevin Riehl** — PhD Researcher, Multimodal AI
- **Ning Wang** — PhD Researcher, Multimodal AI & RL

## Visiting Researcher Program

The lab explicitly offers an **Affiliate / Visiting Researcher Track**:
> "For PhD, postdoc, and industry researchers: Pursue collaborative AI research projects with autonomy and mentorship."

This is directly relevant — they are set up to host visiting researchers.

## Industry & Ecosystem Partners

- Zurich Insurance (core partner for agentic AI in insurance)
- Google DeepMind (co-hosted events)
- Stanford (joint multimodal AI research)
- AWS, Google (cloud credits for projects)

## Selected Publications

- "Practical Considerations for Agentic LLM Systems" (arXiv:2412.04093) — directly on agentic LLM system design
- Publications list: https://las.inf.ethz.ch/publications (affiliated with Learning & Adaptive Systems Group)

## Fit Analysis

| Criterion | Score | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Agentic AI | ★★★★★ | Core focus of the lab |
| Multimodal AI | ★★★★☆ | TSLMs, ALMs, vision-language |
| Cyber range / CTF | ★★☆☆☆ | No explicit work, but education + evaluation frameworks are adjacent |
| Ed tech | ★★★★☆ | Explicit "Agentic AI in Education" PhD researcher; studyflash, taskbase partnerships |
| Erasmus+ feasibility | ★★★☆☆ | Non-EU; requires bilateral agreement check |
| Mentorship | ★★★★☆ | Structured visiting researcher track exists |

## Contact

- Lab website: https://www.agenticsystemslab.org/#contact
- Email via web form on site
- Dr. Robert Jakob, Dr. Kevin O'Sullivan (co-directors)

# Aarhus University — A3 Lab (Adaptive & Agentic AI)

## Overview

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Institution** | Aarhus University |
| **Lab** | A3 Lab — Adaptive & Agentic AI |
| **Website** | https://ece.au.dk/en/research/key-areas-in-research-and-development/signal-processing-and-machine-learning/translate-to-english-research-groups-alt/adaptive-agentic-ai-a3-lab |
| **Country** | Denmark |
| **Erasmus+** | EU ✓ — Denmark is a programme country |
| **Department** | Electrical and Computer Engineering (ECE) |

## Lab Lead

- **Behzad Bozorgtabar** — Associate Professor
  - Email: behzad@ece.au.dk
  - Office: Building 5125, Room 316

## Research Vision

> "A3 Lab develops trustworthy adaptive and agentic AI—especially test-time adaptation and multimodal foundation models—for safe deployment under domain shift in safety-critical real-world applications."

## Research Pillars

### 1. Vision–Language Learning & Reasoning
- Multimodal foundation models (vision-language and beyond)
- Prompting and instruction following
- **Interactive and agentic systems**
- Explainability and faithful reasoning

### 2. Self-Supervised Representation Learning
- Self-supervised learning for cross-task/domain transfer
- Synthetic data and generative modeling for robustness
- Reducing dependence on labeled data

### 3. Open-World, Continual & Test-Time Adaptation
- Domain generalization / test-time adaptation
- Parameter-efficient continual updates
- Unknown-class / novelty recognition
- Handling evolving and recurring domain shifts

### 4. Multi-Agent Orchestration & Efficient Vision
- **Multi-agent coordination and planning** for complex AI pipelines
- **Human–AI teaming** and decision support
- Efficient model design (distillation, efficient transformers, TinyML)
- Rigorous evaluation protocols for reliability under drift

## Selected Publications

- Publications available at: https://ece.au.dk/en/research/key-areas-in-research-and-development/signal-processing-and-machine-learning/translate-to-english-research-groups-alt/adaptive-agentic-ai-a3-lab
- Related broader group: "AI for the people" publication list at https://www.ai.aau.dk/research/publication-list

## Why This Lab Fits

The A3 Lab's focus on **adaptive and agentic AI in safety-critical settings** aligns well with cyber range environments (which are safety-critical training environments). Their work on:
- Multi-agent orchestration → directly applicable to multi-agent CTF scenarios
- Test-time adaptation → relevant for adapting AI agents to new cyber range scenarios
- Human-AI teaming → directly relevant to how learners interact with AI in cyber ranges
- Uncertainty quantification → critical for trustworthy AI in security training

## Fit Analysis

| Criterion | Score | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Agentic AI | ★★★★★ | Core focus — "agentic" in the name |
| Multimodal AI | ★★★★☆ | Vision-language foundation models |
| Cyber range / CTF | ★★☆☆☆ | No explicit work, but safety-critical focus is adjacent |
| Ed tech | ★★☆☆☆ | No explicit ed tech work, but human-AI teaming is relevant |
| Erasmus+ feasibility | ★★★★★ | EU programme country, well-established Erasmus+ partnerships |
| Mentorship | ★★★☆☆ | Associate professor-led group, check team size |

## Contact

- Behzad Bozorgtabar: behzad@ece.au.dk
- Department: https://ece.au.dk/en/about-the-department/contact

arguments.content:
# TU Darmstadt — Multimodal AI Lab (MAI)

## Overview

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Institution** | Technische Universität Darmstadt |
| **Lab** | Multimodal AI Lab (MAI) |
| **Website** | https://www.informatik.tu-darmstadt.de/mai |
| **Country** | Germany |
| **Erasmus+** | EU ✓ — Germany is a programme country |
| **Department** | Computer Science (Dept. 20) |

## Lab Leads

- **Prof. Dr. Anna Rohrbach** — Multimodal AI, Google Scholar: [link](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=GHpxNQIAAAAJ)
- **Prof. Dr. Marcus Rohrbach** — Multimodal AI, Google Scholar: [link](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=3kDtybgAAAAJ)

Both have extensive publication records in top venues (CVPR, ICCV, ECCV, ACL, EMNLP, NeurIPS).

## Research Areas

The lab is organized into two main research directions:

### Multimodal Grounded Learning
- Grounding language in visual perception
- Vision-language pre-training and fine-tuning
- Cross-modal alignment and retrieval
- Visual reasoning and question answering

### Multimodal Reliable AI
- Trustworthiness and robustness of multimodal models
- Hallucination detection and mitigation
- Bias and fairness in multimodal systems
- Out-of-distribution detection and handling

### Multimodal Fact-Checking
- Automated verification of claims using multimodal evidence
- Combining text, image, and knowledge base information for fact-checking
- The lab won the Fact-Checking Challenge

## Team

- >10 dedicated PhD researchers working on diverse topics
- Open positions regularly available

## Selected Publications (Representative)

The Rohrbachs have published extensively on:
- Vision-language models and their reliability
- Multimodal grounded learning
- Video understanding and description
- Visual question answering
- Image captioning and retrieval
- See Google Scholar profiles for complete lists

## Why This Lab Fits

1. **Multimodal AI expertise** — one of Europe's strongest groups for vision-language AI
2. **Reliable AI focus** — trustworthiness and robustness are critical for deploying AI in cyber ranges
3. **Grounded learning** — grounding AI outputs in evidence (relevant for CTF verification, security analysis)
4. **Germany = strong Erasmus+ country**
5. **Hessian AI building** — TU Darmstadt is part of hessian.AI, a major AI center with extensive infrastructure

## Complementary Knowledge You Could Gain

- State-of-the-art multimodal foundation model architectures
- How to make multimodal AI reliable and trustworthy
- Grounding techniques for connecting AI reasoning to verifiable evidence
- Fact-checking methods applicable to security claim verification

## Fit Analysis

| Criterion | Score | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Agentic AI | ★★☆☆☆ | Less explicit; more focused on model architecture than agents |
| Multimodal AI | ★★★★★ | Core focus — one of Europe's top labs |
| Cyber range / CTF | ★☆☆☆☆ | No explicit connection |
| Ed tech | ★☆☆☆☆ | No explicit education work |
| Erasmus+ feasibility | ★★★★★ | EU programme country, large university with many agreements |
| Mentorship | ★★★★☆ | >10 PhD students, two professors, active lab |

## Contact

- Lab website: https://www.informatik.tu-darmstadt.de/mai/multimodal_ai/people_mai/index.en.jsp
- Open positions: https://www.informatik.tu-darmstadt.de/mai/multimodal_ai/jobs_mai/mai_people_jobs.en.jsp

# Imperial College London — Language and Multimodal AI Lab (LAMA)

## Lab Lead

  - Google Scholar: extensive publications in NLP, multimodal ML, machine translation

- **Dr. Julia Ive** — Research focus on machine translation quality estimation
- **Dr. Pranava Madhyastha** — Research on NLP, language understanding
- **Dr. Marek Rei** — Research on NLP, language models
- **Dr. Chiraag Lala** — Multimodal ML research

## Research Focus

The LAMA Lab develops machine learning algorithms for language understanding and generation following a **holistic approach** where language is combined with other modalities. Core areas:

- **Multimodal language generation** — combining text with images, videos, audio, sensor data
- **Machine translation** — quality estimation, post-editing, multilingual systems
- **Image captioning** and visual question answering
- **Transfer learning for language** — cross-lingual and cross-modal transfer
- **NLP for health and sustainable development**
- **Quality evaluation and estimation** — metrics for generated content
- **Language learning applications**

- "MultiSubs: A Large-scale Multimodal and Multilingual Dataset" — LREC 2022
- "MLQE-PE: A Multilingual Quality Estimation and Post-Editing Dataset" — LREC 2022

Full list: https://lama.doc.ic.ac.uk/publications

## Why This Lab Fits

1. **Multimodal NLP expertise** — language + vision/audio/sensor integration
2. **Language learning applications** — directly relevant to ed tech
3. **Quality evaluation** — metrics and evaluation methods applicable to any AI system including cyber range agents
4. **Imperial = world-class** — strong research environment and network

# University of Twente — Pervasive Systems & IST

## Overview

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Institution** | University of Twente |
| **Groups** | Pervasive Systems (EEMCS) + Department of Instructional Technology (IST/BMS) |
| **Website** | https://www.utwente.nl/en/eemcs/ps/ (PS) / https://www.utwente.nl/en/bms/ist/ (IST) |
| **Country** | Netherlands |
| **Erasmus+** | EU ✓ — Netherlands is a programme country |

## Two Complementary Groups

### Pervasive Systems Group (EEMCS)
Research on distributed and intelligent systems with cybersecurity applications:

**Key Projects:**
- **Solving CTFs with multi-agent systems** — Using LLM-based multi-agent systems to solve Capture The Flag challenges. This is the most direct intersection with your interests.
- **AI Orchestration** — Coordinating smart agents together
- **Automated Kill-Chain Extraction for Enhanced Cyber Defense** — Master thesis project
- **Do Agents Dream of Root Shells?** — Partial-credit evaluation of LLM agents in CTF challenges (arXiv:2604.19354)

**Publications:**
- "Multi-Agent Penetration Testing AI for the Web" (arXiv:2508.20816)
- "Solving Capture The Flags (CTFs) with multi-agent systems" (master thesis)

### Department of Instructional Technology (IST/BMS)
Research on AI in education:

**Key Projects:**
- **CLAIR — Collaborative Learning Agent for Interactive Reasoning** — An LLM-based collaborative learning agent (https://clair.chat/)
  - Won the Tools Competition
  - Focus on AI as a social agent to support group learning processes
- **AI as a Social Agent to Support Group Learning Processes** — Current research project

**Research areas:** AI in education, collaborative learning, intelligent tutoring, learning analytics

## Why This Combination Is Exceptional

The University of Twente uniquely combines:
1. **CTF/cybersecurity + multi-agent AI** (Pervasive Systems)
2. **AI in education + collaborative learning agents** (IST)

This is arguably the **closest single-university match** to your exact profile: cyber ranges + ed tech + need for agentic/multimodal AI expertise.

## Selected Publications

- "Do Agents Dream of Root Shells? Partial-Credit Evaluation of LLM Agents in Capture The Flag Challenges" — arXiv:2604.19354
- "Multi-Agent Penetration Testing AI for the Web" — arXiv:2508.20816
- CLAIR publications: https://clair.chat/about/

## Complementary Knowledge You Could Gain

- Multi-agent architectures for cybersecurity tasks (CTF solving, penetration testing)
- LLM agent evaluation in security contexts
- Collaborative learning agent design (CLAIR)
- How to bridge cybersecurity training with AI tutoring

## Fit Analysis

| Criterion | Score | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Agentic AI | ★★★★★ | Multi-agent systems for CTF + AI orchestration |
| Multimodal AI | ★★☆☆☆ | Less explicit, but LLM agents are inherently multimodal-capable |
| Cyber range / CTF | ★★★★★ | Direct CTF-solving with multi-agent systems |
| Ed tech | ★★★★☆ | CLAIR project, IST department expertise |
| Erasmus+ feasibility | ★★★★★ | EU programme country, UT has extensive exchange agreements |
| Mentorship | ★★★★☆ | Two departments to draw from; active thesis projects |

## Contact

- IST Department: https://www.utwente.nl/en/bms/ist/research-projects/current-research-projects/

## Relevance to Your Research

CISPA is one of Europe's leading cybersecurity research centers. Several groups work at the intersection of AI and security, including work on LLM agents in security contexts.

## Key Research Groups

### Prof. Dr. Mario Fritz
- Works on AI security, privacy, and ML
- Publications on compromising autonomous LLM agents
- **"Breaking Agents: Compromising Autonomous LLM Agents through Malfunction Amplification"** — CISPA publication directly on attacking LLM agents

### Related Research Areas at CISPA
- AI security and robustness
- Privacy-preserving ML
- Automated security analysis
- Threat detection and response

Broader CISPA research areas: https://cispa.saarland/en/research/research-areas

1. **LLM agents in security context** — directly studies how autonomous LLM agents can be attacked/compromised

3. **Mutual knowledge transfer** — your cyber range domain knowledge + their LLM agent security expertise
4. **Helmholtz = well-funded** — resources, infrastructure, international network

## Caution

- Focus may be more on offensive security than on education/training applications
- Need to identify a specific group leader willing to host

## Complementary Knowledge You Could Gain

- How LLM agents behave in adversarial security environments

- Threat modeling for agentic AI
- Attack vectors specific to LLM-based agents (prompt injection, malfunction amplification)

arguments.path: internship/11 - University of Bern — Software Engineering Group.md
arguments.content:
# University of Bern — Software Engineering Group

## Overview

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Institution** | University of Bern |
| **Group** | Software Engineering Group |
| **Website** | https://seg.inf.unibe.ch/research/ |
| **Country** | Switzerland |
| **Erasmus+** | Non-EU, but Switzerland participates via bilateral agreements |

## Research Focus on Agentic LLMs

The Software Engineering Group at Bern has made **agentic LLMs** a current research topic. Their work includes:

### Active Research Topics
- **Agentic LLMs** — Current topic T0077
- **Agent-Based Code Repair** — Current topic T0050
- **Agentic AI System for Automated Systematic Literature Reviews** — Current topic T0073
- **RL-based Training for Code in LLMs** — Current topic T0067
- **Exploration of Self-Reflective LLMs for Code** — Current topic T0051

### Key Publication
- **"LLM-Based Agentic Systems for Software Engineering"** — arXiv:2601.09822
  - Comprehensive treatment of how LLM-based agents are applied to software engineering tasks

## Why This Fits

1. **Explicit agentic LLM focus** — one of few groups with "agentic LLMs" as a named research topic
2. **Agent architectures** — their work on agent-based code repair and self-reflective LLMs covers agent design patterns
3. **Switzerland = strong research environment**

## Caution

- Focus is software engineering, not cybersecurity or education
- The agentic LLM work is in the context of code, not cyber ranges
- Switzerland Erasmus+ requires bilateral agreement check

## Complementary Knowledge You Could Gain

- Agent architecture patterns for LLM-based systems
- Self-reflective LLM design (agents that critique their own output)
- RL-based training methods for LLM agents
- Evaluation methodologies for agentic LLM systems

## Fit Analysis

| Criterion | Score | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Agentic AI | ★★★★☆ | Explicit research topic on agentic LLMs |
| Multimodal AI | ★☆☆☆☆ | Not a focus |
| Cyber range / CTF | ★☆☆☆☆ | No explicit connection |
| Ed tech | ★☆☆☆☆ | No explicit connection |
| Erasmus+ feasibility | ★★★☆☆ | Non-EU; bilateral agreement needed |
| Mentorship | ★★☆☆☆ | Small group; check hosting capacity |

## Contact

- Group: https://seg.inf.unibe.ch/research/
- Keywords/LLMs: https://seg.inf.unibe.ch/keywords/llms/

# Know-Center Graz — IACAI (Interfaces of Agent-Centric AI)

## Overview

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Institution** | Know-Center (COMET center, affiliated with TU Graz) |
| **Module** | IACAI — Interfaces of Agent-Centric AI |
| **Website** | https://www.know-center.at/en/research/comet-modul/interfaces-of-agent-centric-ai-iacai/ |
| **Country** | Austria |
| **Erasmus+** | EU ✓ — Austria is a programme country |
| **Location** | Graz, Austria |

## Research Focus

IACAI is a COMET module (Competence Centers for Excellent Technologies) focused on:

- **Agent-centric AI** — designing AI systems from the perspective of agents and their interfaces
- **Human-agent interfaces** — how humans interact with and control AI agents
- **Agent orchestration** — coordination of multiple AI agents
- **Trust and reliability** in agent-based systems

## Broader Context: Know-Center

The Know-Center is Austria's leading research center for data-driven business and AI:
- COMET-funded (Austrian excellence program)
- Strong industry partnerships
- Focus on applied AI research

## Related: SBA Research / AIT LLM4CTI

In the Austrian AI-cybersecurity ecosystem, two additional projects are relevant:

### LLM4CTI — LLMs for Cyber Threat Intelligence
- **AIT Austrian Institute of Technology** + **SBA Research**
- https://www.ait.ac.at/en/research-topics/cyber-security/projects/llm4cti
- https://www.sba-research.org/research/projects/llm4cti/
- Uses LLMs for automated cyber threat intelligence extraction
- Funded by netidee program

### MOSAICO Project
- "Management, Orchestration and Supervision of AI-agent Communities"
- http://mosaico-project.eu/
- EU project on orchestrating communities of AI agents

## Why This Fits

1. **Agent-centric AI** — explicit focus on agent design and interfaces
2. **Austrian ecosystem** — access to LLM4CTI (AIT/SBA) and MOSAICO for the cybersecurity angle
3. **COMET = well-funded applied research** — structured hosting programs
4. **Austria = EU Erasmus+ country**

## Complementary Knowledge You Could Gain

- Agent-centric design principles
- Human-agent interface patterns
- Agent orchestration architectures
- (Via AIT/SBA) LLMs for cyber threat intelligence

## Fit Analysis

| Criterion | Score | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Agentic AI | ★★★★☆ | Core focus — "agent-centric AI" |
| Multimodal AI | ★★☆☆☆ | Not explicitly stated |
| Cyber range / CTF | ★★★☆☆ | Via AIT/SBA LLM4CTI ecosystem |
| Ed tech | ★☆☆☆☆ | Not a focus |
| Erasmus+ feasibility | ★★★★★ | EU programme country |
| Mentorship | ★★★☆☆ | COMET center; check for visitor programs |

## Contact

- Know-Center: https://www.know-center.at/en/research/comet-modul/interfaces-of-agent-centric-ai-iacai/
- AIT LLM4CTI: https://www.ait.ac.at/en/research-topics/cyber-security/projects/llm4cti

arguments.path: internship/13 - University of Edinburgh — Autonomous Agents & ELIAI.md
arguments.content:
# University of Edinburgh — Autonomous Agents & ELIAI

## Overview

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Institution** | University of Edinburgh |
| **Groups** | Autonomous Agents Research Group + ELIAI Lab |
| **Websites** | https://agents.inf.ed.ac.uk/ / https://informatics.ed.ac.uk/eliai |
| **Country** | United Kingdom |
| **Erasmus+** | Post-Brexit — check eligibility; UK has Turing Scheme as replacement |
| **Department** | School of Informatics |

## Autonomous Agents Research Group

One of the longest-established agent research groups in Europe.

### Research Areas
- Multi-agent systems
- Autonomous agent architectures
- Agent planning and reasoning
- LLM-based agents
- Dynamic planning with LLMs

### Selected Publications
- "Dynamic planning with an LLM" — https://www.research.ed.ac.uk/en/publications/dynamic-planning-with-an-llm/
- "Towards Reducible Uncertainty Modeling for Reliable Large Language Model Agents" — arXiv:2602.05073
- "Practical Considerations for Agentic LLM Systems" — arXiv:2412.04093 (shared with ETH ASL)

### Related PhD Position
- **PhD: Uncertainty-Aware Agentic AI** — SPADS CDT at Edinburgh
  - Focus on making agentic AI systems uncertainty-aware
  - Directly relevant to trustworthy AI in security contexts

## ELIAI Lab

- **Edinburgh Laboratory for Integrated AI**
- Research on integrated AI systems, combining multiple AI capabilities
- https://informatics.ed.ac.uk/eliai/about

## Additional: Multimodal Generative AI for Embodied Collaborative Agents

Edinburgh also offers a PhD studentship on **"Multimodal Generative AI for Embodied Collaborative Agents"**:
- https://informatics.ed.ac.uk/study-with-us/our-degrees/postgraduate-research-and-cdts/phd-multimodal-generative-ai-collaborative-agents
- Combines multimodal AI + collaborative agents + embodied AI

## Why This Fits

1. **Established agent research** — one of Europe's oldest and most respected groups
2. **LLM agent planning** — dynamic planning with LLMs directly relevant to cyber range agents
3. **Uncertainty-aware agentic AI** — critical for reliable deployment in security contexts
4. **Multimodal + collaborative agents** — available through other Edinburgh researchers
5. **Edinburgh = world-class AI school**

## Caution

- Post-Brexit Erasmus+ eligibility requires verification
- UK has replaced Erasmus+ with the Turing Scheme — different process
- Edinburgh is extremely competitive; hosting may depend on finding a willing supervisor

## Complementary Knowledge You Could Gain

- Classical + modern agent architectures (Edinburgh has deep history)
- LLM agent planning and reasoning
- Uncertainty quantification for agent reliability
- Multi-agent coordination patterns

## Fit Analysis

| Criterion | Score | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Agentic AI | ★★★★★ | Core focus — one of Europe's top agent groups |
| Multimodal AI | ★★★☆☆ | Available via other groups; not core to agents group |
| Cyber range / CTF | ★★☆☆☆ | Not explicit, but agent reliability work is relevant |
| Ed tech | ★☆☆☆☆ | Not a focus |
| Erasmus+ feasibility | ★★☆☆☆ | Post-Brexit; Turing Scheme or specific agreements needed |
| Mentorship | ★★★☆☆ | World-class researchers; check availability for hosting |

## Contact

- Autonomous Agents: https://agents.inf.ed.ac.uk/
- ELIAI: https://informatics.ed.ac.uk/eliai

Done. 14 files in `internship/`: a central dashboard (README.md) and 13 individual institution pages.

**Top 5 picks for your profile** (cyber ranges + ed tech background, need agentic/multimodal AI):

1. **ETH Zürich — Agentic Systems Lab** — has an explicit "Agentic AI in Education" PhD track, structured visiting researcher program, and covers agentic + multimodal + evaluation. Best all-around fit despite non-EU Erasmus+ complication.

2. **University of Twente** — the closest single-university match: Pervasive Systems builds multi-agent CTF solvers, while IST builds AI tutoring agents (CLAIR). Your cyber range expertise would be directly complementary.

3. **Aarhus University — A3 Lab** — strong agentic AI + multimodal foundation models + test-time adaptation, clean EU Erasmus+, safety-critical focus that maps to security contexts.

4. **CISPA Helmholtz** — if you want to lean into the cybersecurity angle: they publish on compromising autonomous LLM agents, and your cyber range knowledge + their LLM-agent security expertise would be a strong two-way exchange.

5. **University of Würzburg — BRACE-LLM** — blue/red LLM agents for cybersecurity. Narrower but the most directly on-point project for "agentic AI in cyber range contexts."

Edificio C - Segunda planta · Facultad de Informática · Edificio Pleiades

[Meet the team](https://portalinvestigacion.um.es/grupos/206810/detalle?lang=en)

Research areas

Clear visibility of key cyber environments to enable better decision-making

Real-time analysis of assets complexity and evolution

Anticipation of threats, adversary strategies, and operational risks

Faster, more effective mission decisions powered by high-value information

content.thinking:
Excellent! Now I have key information. The CyberDataLab already has partnerships with:
- **ETH Zürich** — listed as an academic partner!
- **AIT Austrian Institute of Technology** — also listed as a partner (relevant for the Know-Center Graz / LLM4CTI lead)

The research line "Cyber Ranges and Educational Technology" explicitly mentions:
- AI-powered modeling of human and adversarial behavior through NPCs, APT simulations
- Multimodal learning analytics and LLM-based tutors
- LLM-based tutors to assess skills, engagement, and performance in real time

And the "Artificial Intelligence for Cyberdefence" line mentions:
- LLM-driven reporting and automated countermeasure deployment

Now let me search for joint publications between CyberDataLab researchers and the proposed groups.

content.thinking:
Now I have the key researchers at CyberDataLab. The leader is Gregorio Martinez Perez. Other notable researchers include:
- Felix Gomez Marmol
- Felix Jesus Garcia Clemente
- Javier Pastor Galindo
- Jose Maria Jorquera Valero
- Pedro Beltran Lopez
- Alberto Huertas Celdran
- Pantaleone Nespoli
- Jose Antonio Ruiperez Valiente

The website also reveals ETH Zürich and AIT Austrian Institute of Technology as academic partners.

Now let me search for joint publications between CyberDataLab researchers and the candidate institutions.

arguments.query: "Gomez Marmol" OR "Martinez Perez" OR "Garcia Clemente" OR "Pastor Galindo" OR "Huertas Celdran" OR "Nespoli" "ETH" OR "Aarhus" OR "Twente" OR "Darmstadt" OR "Imperial" cybersecurity

Title: Gregorio Martinez Perez's Web Page

URL Source: https://webs.um.es/gregorio/publications.html

Markdown Content:
_Last updated: October 2022_

**Publication profiles**

 My publication profile is available from these different sources (listed in alphabetical order): 
*   **DBLP** [[![Image 1](https://webs.um.es/gregorio/icon-web.gif)](http://www.informatik.uni-trier.de/~ley/pers/hy/p/P=eacute=rez:Gregorio_Mart=iacute=nez.html)]

Title: EUropean cyber INFormation Warfare Toolbox

URL Source: https://eucinf.eu/partners.html

Markdown Content:
## Partners

![Image 1](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/Logos_Partners_White/PNG/Airbus.png)

![Image 2](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/banderas/fr.png)

### [Airbus Defence and Space](https://www.airbus.com/en/products-services/defence)

Airbus Defence and Space brings interoperable and collaborative solutions to ensure superiority for combat systems across the five operational domains: land, air, sea, space and cyber. It is in particular a major actor in the intelligence domain, from high resolution satellite imagery to Joint ISR and Multi-Intelligence solutions. Its customer-centric and innovative systems allow collation, processing, analysis and refining of available information, to give decision-makers the ability to quickly see situation or detail overview. Airbus Defence and Space has decades of experience in intelligence integration and references from key ministries across Europe and around the globe

![Image 3](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/Logos_Partners_White/PNG/Airbus.png)

![Image 4](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/banderas/fr.png)

### [Airbus CyberSecurity](https://www.airbus.com/en/products-services/defence/cyber-security)

Airbus CyberSecurity, a fully integrated part of Airbus Defence and Space, is a European leader in cybersecurity for the aerospace and defense sectors. With over 450 cyber professionals based in France, Germany, the UK, and Spain, the company provides custom cybersecurity solutions to defense, governmental, and institutional clients. Their offerings include designing, developing, integrating, and deploying cyber security measures along with ongoing operational support throughout the lifecycle of systems. Recognized for its unique expertise in platform security and embedded systems, Airbus CyberSecurity utilizes cutting-edge technologies to address the increasing and evolving cyber threats, ensuring robust protection for their clients and their systems

![Image 5](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/Logos_Partners_White/PNG/Airbus.png)

![Image 6](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/banderas/alemania.png)

### [Airbus Germany](https://www.airbus.com/)

Airbus Germany plays a crucial role within Airbus' global operations, focusing on commercial aircraft and military equipment manufacturing across 27 sites. Central to its activities is the Hamburg location, pivotal for the development and production of Airbus jetliners, including the A320 family. This site is noted for its advanced manufacturing techniques, featuring highly automated assembly lines equipped with the latest in robotic technology. Airbus Germany is also prominent in the rotorcraft sector, particularly at Donauwörth, which produces a significant portion of Airbus jetliners' doors globally​

![Image 7](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/Logos_Partners_White/PNG/anozr_way.png)

![Image 8](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/banderas/fr.png)

### [Anzorway](https://anzorway.com/)

Anzorway is a French startup specializing in software development focused on protecting individuals against cyber risks. Founded in Rennes in 2019 by Alban Ondrejeck, a former officer in the French intelligence services, and Philippe Luc, a former executive in the insurance sector, Anzorway has developed an award-winning proprietary technology based on Artificial Intelligence. Anzorway's software solutions enable corporate leaders and their teams to control their digital footprint to guard against threats such as social engineering, identity theft, espionage, ransomware, and data theft. With an initial funding of €2 million in 2021 from BPI, Breizh Up, and BNP Development, Anzorway is in an acceleration phase, employs 35 staff members, and aims for 300% growth

![Image 9](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/Logos_Partners_White/PNG/AIT.png)

![Image 10](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/banderas/austri.png)

### [Austrian Institute of Technology](https://www.ait.ac.at/en)

The AIT Austrian Institute of Technology is Austria’s largest non-university research institute. With its seven Centers, AIT regards itself as a highly specialised research and development partner for industry. AIT provides research and technological development to realize basic innovations for the next generation of infrastructure related technologies in the fields of Energy, Transport Technologies, Health & Bioresources, Digital Safety & Security, Vision, Automation & Control and Technology Experience. AIT’s Center for Digital Safety & Security is frequent collaboration partner as well as coordinator of large-scale EDF projects. https://www.ait.ac.at/en/​

![Image 11](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/Logos_Partners_White/PNG/Approach_cyber.png)

![Image 12](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/banderas/belgica.png)

### [Approach](https://www.approach.be/)

Approach is a Belgian company specializing in cybersecurity and risk management. Founded in 2001, Approach provides solutions and services that cover consultancy, systems integration, auditing, and training. With a focus on information protection and security, Approach works with clients from various industries, including banking, telecommunications, and the public sector. Its main headquarters are located in Louvain-la-Neuve, Belgium.

![Image 13](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/Logos_Partners_White/PNG/Bloom.png)

![Image 14](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/banderas/fr.png)

### [Bloom](https://bloomsocialanalytics.com/en/)

Bloom has become a leader in risk detection and analysis in social media. The platform covers a diverse set of use cases, such as detection of influence operations and hostile accounts, as well as OSINT and geopolitical strategy. Bloom clients are large corporations, government institutions, as well as military and security organisations. Bloom has been heavily investing in the development of proprietary technology that provides the most powerful data collection and analytics capabilities in its sector. Moreover, while our focus has been on analysing social media, the underlying data and analysis models we have built are generic and can be easily adopted and optimised for other data sources

![Image 15](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/Logos_Partners_White/PNG/BV_Tech.png)

![Image 16](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/banderas/it.png)

### [BV TECH](https://www.bv-tech.it/)

BV TECH is an Italian group specializing in information and communication technologies (ICT), cybersecurity, and consulting. Founded in 2005, BV TECH operates in sectors such as defense, space, energy, and healthcare. The company offers services ranging from strategic consulting and project management to software development and systems integration. Its headquarters are in Rome, Italy.

![Image 17](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/Logos_Partners_White/PNG/cybernetica.png)

![Image 18](https://eucinf.eu/partners.html)![Image 19](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/banderas/estonia.png)

### [Cybernetica](https://cyber.ee/)

CYB is an R&D intensive ICT company with ca. 120 employees. Cybernetica has vast and unique experience of building future-proof technologies that rely on R&D and has been part of various research and/or development projects focusing on the defence industry, information security and mission critical systems around the world. .

![Image 20](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/Logos_Partners_White/PNG/CyEx.png)

![Image 21](https://eucinf.eu/partners.html)![Image 22](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/banderas/cyprus.png)

### [](https://eucinf.eu/partners.html#)CyEX

CyEx develops capabilities for its partners who build secure societies and larger-scale critical systems. CyEx is an innovation-focused cybersecurity service provider that offers proactive cyber services and solutions utilising cutting-edge AI and ML technologies primarily for large international suppliers in the energy and defence sectors, as well as for governments and European institutions. CyEx specialises in creating prevention-oriented military-grade cybersecurity as well as proactive protection and defence solutions for critical industries. CyEx’s in-house AI and ML research lab is dedicated to pushing the boundaries of innovation and always delivering the most innovative and effective solutions possible to its clients.

![Image 23](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/Logos_Partners_White/PNG/eclecticla.png)

![Image 24](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/banderas/netherlands.png)

### [EclecticIQ](https://www.eclecticiq.com/)

EclecticIQ is a Dutch cybersecurity company founded in 2014, specializing in threat intelligence, hunting, and response technology and services. The company is headquartered in Amsterdam, Netherlands, and employs around 79 people. EclecticIQ's platform integrates and interprets intelligence data from various sources to help organizations detect, prevent, and respond to cyber threats. This includes open sources, commercial suppliers, and industry partnerships, making it a comprehensive solution for cybersecurity analysts.

![Image 25](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/Logos_Partners_White/PNG/Indra.png)

![Image 26](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/banderas/sp.png)

### [Indra](https://www.indracompany.com/)

Indra is one of the leading global technology and consulting companies and the technological partner for core business operations of its customers world-wide, with operations in over 140 countries. Currently leading the ECYSAP EDIDP related project and previous actions, and its integration to real National EU defence infrastructures.

![Image 27](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/Logos_Partners_White/PNG/Forward.png)

![Image 28](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/banderas/belgica.png)

### [Forward Global](https://www.forward-global.com/)

Forward Global designs and delivers services and technologies to manage digital, economic, and information risks. The group supports our clients at a national and international level:Cyber risk management and when responding to attacks targeting any and all mission-critical systems, managing critical issues (such as negotiations, litigation and disputes, restructuring) during growth operations (including M&A, international expansion, major projects, governance changes) when strategic positions need to be defined (communications, government affairs, digital, etc.) and in responding to crises (fraud, counterfeiting, piracy, data leaks)

![Image 29](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/Logos_Partners_White/PNG/hensoldt.png)

![Image 30](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/banderas/austri.png)

### [Hendsoldt](https://www.hensoldt-analytics.com/)

HENSOLDT Analytics develops solutions to mine traditional as well as social media contents in multiple languages from a wide portfolio of (openly accessible) sources. This includes the collection and indexing of audio, video, images and text by automatic speech recognition, various NLP technologies for enrichment as well as a set of visual processing technologies. The solutions allow to process and search large collections of media contents in near real-time and offer a variety of analytics capabilities. HENSOLDT supports commercial applications (e.g., news monitoring, broadcast news indexing) as well as government applications (e.g. OSINT, public opinion monitoring, surveillance and intelligence gathering).

![Image 31](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/Logos_Partners_White/PNG/Leonardo.png)

![Image 32](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/banderas/it.png)

### [Leonardo](https://www.leonardocompany.com/)

Leonardo is a global high-tech player in the Aerospace, Defence and Security. It designs and develops products, services, and integrated dual-use solutions for governments, Armed Forces and institutions, covering every possible operating scenario: air and land, naval and maritime, space and cyberspace.

![Image 33](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/Logos_Partners_White/PNG/sahar.png)

![Image 34](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/banderas/fr.png)

### [](https://sahar.fr/)Sahar

Sahar specializes in the collection, analysis, and visualization of massive datasets, offering cyber solutions that can integrate into existing workflows or operate independently. Located in Paris, the company is dedicated to advancing French technological expertise in cyber solutions and data science. Sahar provides advisory services to guide both private and public entities through complex strategic data projects, emphasizing a flexible, innovative approach to data science including AI, machine learning, and deep learning.

![Image 35](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/Logos_Partners_White/PNG/space.png)

![Image 36](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/banderas/grecia.jpg)

### [Space Hellas](https://www.space.gr/)

Space Hellas is a Greek company offering integrated solutions and services in information and communication technologies (ICT). Founded in 1985, Space Hellas specializes in telecommunications networks, cybersecurity, cloud services, and infrastructure management. The company serves clients in sectors such as banking, government, defense, and telecommunications, providing solutions tailored to the specific needs of each industry. Its main headquarters are in Athens, Greece.

![Image 37](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/Logos_Partners_White/PNG/storyzy.png)

![Image 38](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/banderas/fr.png)

### [Storyzy](https://www.storyzy.com/)

Storyzy is a French tech company that developed an OSINT tool designed specifically for defense, security, and foreign affairs professionals. Storyzy’s tool is designed to monitor and investigate malign manipulation activities, foreign interference and disinformation. It enables quantitative measurements and qualitative assessments for in-depth and granular analyses

![Image 39](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/Logos_Partners_White/PNG/tilde.png)

![Image 40](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/banderas/latvia.png)

### [Tilde](https://www.tilde.com/)

Tilde is a company that specializes in language technology solutions, focusing on breaking down language barriers for global communication. Founded in 1991, Tilde is headquartered in Riga, Latvia, and provides a range of services including machine and website translation, AI-powered chatbots, speech transcription, and localization services. The company is well-regarded for its work in supporting both large and small languages, particularly those with complex and highly inflected structures, which are often overlooked by mainstream language technologies.

![Image 41](https://eucinf.eu/assets/img-euCinf/Logos_Partners_White/PNG/Universidad_Murcia.png)

### [Universidad de Murcia](https://www.umu.es/)

UMU provides high expertise in terms of research and transfer of results and knowledge to industry. The more than 40 members of the Cybersecurity and Data Science Lab (CyberDataLab, https://cyberdatalab.um.es/), belonging to the UMU’s Faculty of Computer Science, have a wide experience in diverse topics related to EUCINF project, such as disinformation, threat modelling, artificial intelligence, cybersecurity and cyberintelligence (OSINT, DARKINT, SOCMINT).

UPM is the oldest and largest of the Spanish Technical Universities. UPM currently has more than 3000 faculty members, around 38.000 undergraduate students, and around 6.000 postgraduate students. UPM’s Schools cover most Engineering disciplines, with a strong commitment to R&D and Innovation. UPM has more than 200 Research Units and several Research Institutes and Technological Centres in a variety of areas, including cybersecurity and cyberdefence

### [8Bells](https://www.8bellsresearch.com/)

Eight Bells (8BELLS) is an independent Research and Consulting company specializing in selected parts of Information and Communication Technologies (ICT) as well as in modelling and analysis for businesses. arguments.content:
# CyberDataLab (UMU) — Connection Analysis with Candidate Institutions

## Your Research Group

- **Group:** CyberDataLab — Cybersecurity and Data Science Laboratory
- **University:** Universidad de Murcia (UMU)
- **Leader:** Gregorio Martinez Perez
- **Key Researchers (EdTech/Cyber Range line):** Felix Gomez Marmol, Felix Jesus Garcia Clemente, Javier Pastor Galindo, Jose Maria Jorquera Valero, Alberto Huertas Celdran, Pantaleone Nespoli, Pedro Beltran Lopez, Jose Antonio Ruiperez Valiente

---

## Confirmed Connections

### 1. AIT Austrian Institute of Technology — STRONG CONNECTION

| Evidence | Details |
|---|---|
| **Academic partner** | Listed on CyberDataLab website as academic partner |
| **EUCINF project** | Both AIT and UMU are partners in the EUCINF (EUropean cyber INFormation Warfare Toolbox) project. AIT coordinates EDF projects and is a frequent collaboration partner. |
| **LLM4CTI** | AIT leads the LLM4CTI project (LLMs for Cyber Threat Intelligence), which is directly relevant to your agentic AI + cybersecurity interest |

**Implication:** Your tutor (Gregorio Martinez Perez) can credibly write a recommendation letter citing the existing AIT collaboration. The LLM4CTI project at AIT is a natural bridge to the Know-Center / IACAI group in Graz (same Austrian ecosystem). AIT's Center for Digital Safety & Security is the contact point.

**Erasmus+ route:** Austria = EU programme country. Straightforward.

---

### 2. Saarland University / CISPA Helmholtz — CONFIRMED JOINT PUBLICATIONS

| Evidence | Details |
|---|---|
| **Joint publications** | UMU's research portal lists collaboration publications with Saarland University (organization ID 1288) |
| **CyberSec4Europe** | Both UMU and CISPA/Saarland participated in the CyberSec4Europe EU project |

**Implication:** There is a documented publication history between UMU and Saarland University researchers. CISPA is affiliated with Saarland University, so the Erasmus+ agreement would go through Saarland.

**Erasmus+ route:** Germany = EU programme country. Through Saarland University.

---

### 3. TU Darmstadt — CONFIRMED COLLABORATION RECORD

| Evidence | Details |
|---|---|
| **Collaboration page** | UMU's research portal has a dedicated collaboration page with "Technical University of Darmstadt" (organization ID 947) with joint publications |

**Implication:** There is a formal collaboration record. However, note that the MAI (Multimodal AI) Lab may not be the specific group involved — the existing collaboration might be with other TU Darmstadt departments. You would need to check if the joint publications involve MAI Lab researchers specifically.

**Erasmus+ route:** Germany = EU programme country. TU Darmstadt is a large university with many exchange agreements.

---

### 4. ETH Zürich — LISTED AS ACADEMIC PARTNER

| Evidence | Details |
|---|---|
| **Academic partner** | Listed on CyberDataLab website as an academic partner (logo shown) |
| **No joint publications found** | In Gregorio Martinez Perez's publication list (last updated 2022), no direct co-authorship with ETH researchers was found |

**Implication:** The partnership exists at the institutional/project level. Your tutor can reference this partnership in a recommendation letter, but the connection is less concrete than the AIT or Saarland relationships.

**Erasmus+ route:** Switzerland participates in Erasmus+ through bilateral agreements. UMU likely has an agreement given the listed partnership.

---

### 5. University of Zurich (UZH) — EXISTING CO-AUTHORSHIP (Not in original candidate list)

| Evidence | Details |
|---|---|
| **Alberto Huertas Celdran at UZH** | Huertas Celdran has been affiliated with UZH's Communication Systems Group |
| **Co-authored publications** | Multiple papers co-authored with Burkhard Stiller and Gérôme Bovet from UZH |
| **CyberDFL project** | UZH hosts the CyberDFL project with UMU involvement |

**Implication:** While UZH is not the same as ETH, both are in Zurich. The existing UZH collaboration could facilitate introductions to ETH groups. Consider also whether UZH has relevant AI/agent research groups.

---

## No Direct Connections Found

The following candidate institutions showed **no evidence** of existing collaboration with CyberDataLab:

- **Aarhus University (A3 Lab)** — No joint publications or project partnerships found
- **SDU Odense** — No joint publications or project partnerships found
- **Imperial College London (LAMA)** — No joint publications or project partnerships found
- **University of Twente** — No joint publications or project partnerships found
- **University of Würzburg** — No joint publications or project partnerships found
- **Utrecht University** — No joint publications or project partnerships found
- **University of Tübingen / IWM** — No joint publications or project partnerships found
- **University of Bern** — No joint publications or project partnerships found
- **University of Edinburgh** — No joint publications or project partnerships found

**Note:** Absence of evidence is not evidence of absence. These results are based on web searches and the UMU research portal. There may be informal connections, conference collaborations, or recent projects not yet indexed.

---

## Recommendation Priority (Updated with Connection Data)

Considering both **research fit** and **existing connections**:

| Rank | Institution | Research Fit | Connection Strength | Recommendation Letter Ease |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **CISPA / Saarland** | ★★★★★ (cybersec + LLM agents) | ★★★★★ (joint publications) | Very easy — documented co-authors |
| 2 | **Know-Center / AIT (Austria)** | ★★★★☆ (agentic AI + LLM4CTI via AIT) | ★★★★★ (EUCINF project partner) | Very easy — active project partner |
| 3 | **ETH Zürich (Agentic Systems Lab)** | ★★★★★ (agentic + multimodal + ed) | ★★★☆☆ (listed partner, no co-pubs) | Easy — can cite partnership |
| 4 | **TU Darmstadt (MAI Lab)** | ★★★★☆ (multimodal AI) | ★★★☆☆ (institutional collaboration) | Moderate — need to check if MAI-specific |
| 5 | **University of Twente** | ★★★★★ (CTF + AI education) | ★☆☆☆☆ (no connection found) | Harder — cold contact |

---

## Actionable Next Steps

1. **Ask your tutor** (Gregorio Martinez Perez) specifically about:
   - The nature of the ETH Zürich partnership (which ETH group, which project)
   - Who at Saarland/CISPA they have co-authored with
   - Whether the AIT/Austria connection via EUCINF could lead to a research stay
   - Whether the TU Darmstadt collaboration involves CS/AI faculty

2. **Cold applications** (no existing connection) will be strongest for:
   - University of Twente (best research fit for your exact profile)
   - Aarhus A3 Lab (strong agentic AI, EU country)

3. **Warm applications** (existing connection) will be strongest for:
   - CISPA/Saarland (documented joint publications)
   - AIT/Know-Center Austria (EUCINF project partner)
   - ETH Zürich (listed partner)

displayContent.text:
# CISPA Helmholtz Center — AI & Cybersecurity

## Overview

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Institution** | CISPA Helmholtz Center for Information Security |
| **Type** | Helmholtz Association research center (associated with Saarland University) |
| **Website** | https://cispa.de/en/research/research-groups |
| **Country** | Germany |
| **Erasmus+** | EU ✓ — via association with Saarland University |
| **Location** | Saarbrücken, Germany |

## Relevance to Your Research

CISPA is one of Europe's leading cybersecurity research centers. Several groups work at the intersection of AI and security, including work on LLM agents in security contexts.

## Key Research Groups

### Prof. Dr. Mario Fritz
- Works on AI security, privacy, and ML
- Publications on compromising autonomous LLM agents
- **"Breaking Agents: Compromising Autonomous LLM Agents through Malfunction Amplification"** — CISPA publication directly on attacking LLM agents

### Related Research Areas at CISPA
- AI security and robustness
- Privacy-preserving ML
- Automated security analysis
- Threat detection and response

## Selected Publications

- "Breaking Agents: Compromising Autonomous LLM Agents through Malfunction Amplification" — https://cispa.saarland/en/research/publications/84125-breaking-agents-compromising-autonomous-llm-agents-through-malfunction-amplification

Broader CISPA research areas: https://cispa.saarland/en/research/research-areas

## Why CISPA Fits

1. **LLM agents in security context** — directly studies how autonomous LLM agents can be attacked/compromised
2. **World-class cybersecurity** — you bring cyber range expertise, they bring AI-for-security expertise
3. **Mutual knowledge transfer** — your cyber range domain knowledge + their LLM agent security expertise
4. **Helmholtz = well-funded** — resources, infrastructure, international network

## Caution

- CISPA is a research center, not directly a university — Erasmus+ agreements would go through Saarland University
- Focus may be more on offensive security than on education/training applications
- Need to identify a specific group leader willing to host

## Complementary Knowledge You Could Gain

- How LLM agents behave in adversarial security environments
- Security evaluation frameworks for autonomous AI systems
- Threat modeling for agentic AI
- Attack vectors specific to LLM-based agents (prompt injection, malfunction amplification)

## Fit Analysis

| Criterion | Score | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Agentic AI | ★★★★☆ | Explicit work on LLM agents in security |
| Multimodal AI | ★★☆☆☆ | Some work, not core focus |
| Cyber range / CTF | ★★★★★ | Core domain — information security center |
| Ed tech | ★☆☆☆☆ | Not their focus |
| Erasmus+ feasibility | ★★★☆☆ | Via Saarland University; needs verification |
| Mentorship | ★★★☆☆ | Depends on specific group; Helmholtz has structured programs |

## Contact

- Research groups: https://cispa.de/en/research/research-groups
- Prof. Mario Fritz: https://cispa.saarland/en/people/mario.fritz

displayContent.text:
# Know-Center Graz — IACAI (Interfaces of Agent-Centric AI)

## Overview

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Institution** | Know-Center (COMET center, affiliated with TU Graz) |
| **Module** | IACAI — Interfaces of Agent-Centric AI |
| **Website** | https://www.know-center.at/en/research/comet-modul/interfaces-of-agent-centric-ai-iacai/ |
| **Country** | Austria |
| **Erasmus+** | EU ✓ — Austria is a programme country |
| **Location** | Graz, Austria |

## Research Focus

IACAI is a COMET module (Competence Centers for Excellent Technologies) focused on:

- **Agent-centric AI** — designing AI systems from the perspective of agents and their interfaces
- **Human-agent interfaces** — how humans interact with and control AI agents
- **Agent orchestration** — coordination of multiple AI agents
- **Trust and reliability** in agent-based systems

## Broader Context: Know-Center

The Know-Center is Austria's leading research center for data-driven business and AI:
- COMET-funded (Austrian excellence program)
- Strong industry partnerships
- Focus on applied AI research

## Related: SBA Research / AIT LLM4CTI

In the Austrian AI-cybersecurity ecosystem, two additional projects are relevant:

### LLM4CTI — LLMs for Cyber Threat Intelligence
- **AIT Austrian Institute of Technology** + **SBA Research**
- https://www.ait.ac.at/en/research-topics/cyber-security/projects/llm4cti
- https://www.sba-research.org/research/projects/llm4cti/
- Uses LLMs for automated cyber threat intelligence extraction
- Funded by netidee program

### MOSAICO Project
- "Management, Orchestration and Supervision of AI-agent Communities"
- http://mosaico-project.eu/
- EU project on orchestrating communities of AI agents

## Why This Fits

1. **Agent-centric AI** — explicit focus on agent design and interfaces
2. **Austrian ecosystem** — access to LLM4CTI (AIT/SBA) and MOSAICO for the cybersecurity angle
3. **COMET = well-funded applied research** — structured hosting programs
4. **Austria = EU Erasmus+ country**

## Complementary Knowledge You Could Gain

- Agent-centric design principles
- Human-agent interface patterns
- Agent orchestration architectures
- (Via AIT/SBA) LLMs for cyber threat intelligence

## Fit Analysis

| Criterion | Score | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Agentic AI | ★★★★☆ | Core focus — "agent-centric AI" |
| Multimodal AI | ★★☆☆☆ | Not explicitly stated |
| Cyber range / CTF | ★★★☆☆ | Via AIT/SBA LLM4CTI ecosystem |
| Ed tech | ★☆☆☆☆ | Not a focus |
| Erasmus+ feasibility | ★★★★★ | EU programme country |
| Mentorship | ★★★☆☆ | COMET center; check for visitor programs |

## Contact

- Know-Center: https://www.know-center.at/en/research/comet-modul/interfaces-of-agent-centric-ai-iacai/
- AIT LLM4CTI: https://www.ait.ac.at/en/research-topics/cyber-security/projects/llm4cti
